tagline: Counter sushi on Victory Boulevard in Glendale.

hero.eyebrow: GLENDALE · VICTORY BLVD · SUSHI · OMAKASE · 115 REVIEWS
hero.heading: Victory Boulevard sushi counter.
hero.subheading: A counter-style sushi shop on Victory Boulevard in Glendale. Edomae nigiri made one piece at a time, sashimi cut to order, a short rotating special list driven by what came in that morning. Reservations recommended for the bar.

about.label: the counter
about.heading: Counter. Itamae. Patience.
paragraphs: Sushi Nishi Ya is a small counter on Victory Boulevard in Glendale. Twelve seats at the bar, four tables, one itamae. The fish comes in fresh, the rice is seasoned twenty minutes before service, the wasabi is grated when you order omakase.

We do classic Edomae nigiri — bluefin tuna, salmon, yellowtail, sea bream, mackerel, sweet shrimp, eel, scallop. The omakase is twelve pieces, chef's selection, paced through the meal. Sashimi plates and a short list of cooked dishes round out the menu.
Open six days a week. Closed Monday. Reservations strongly recommended for the bar. The 115 reviews come from regulars who eat omakase here on date night and special occasions.

services.title: Omakase · 12 pieces
services.description: Chef's selection, paced through the meal. Twelve nigiri, miso soup, hand roll to close. Bar seating only. Recommended.

services.title: Nigiri Set · 8 pieces
services.description: Eight pieces of seasonal nigiri, miso soup. The starter set for the curious. Daily selection.

services.title: Sashimi Combo
services.description: Twelve pieces of sashimi, three varieties, daikon, shiso, fresh wasabi. Daily catch driven.

services.title: Hand Roll Combo
services.description: Three hand rolls — spicy tuna, salmon avocado, yellowtail scallion — miso soup. Lighter sit-down option.

services.title: Bento · Lunch
services.description: Six pieces nigiri, sashimi salad, miso soup, rice, pickle. Lunch only, eleven to two-thirty.

services.title: Chef's Tasting · 16 pieces
services.description: Extended omakase — sixteen pieces, two cooked dishes, dessert. Two-hour reservation. Bar only.

reviews.name: Tomoko I.
reviews.text: Sushi Nishi Ya is the only sushi counter I trust in Glendale. The itamae actually pays attention to which piece goes when — soft fish first, fattier as you go, eel at the end. The rice is the right temperature. I sit at the bar every other Friday.

reviews.name: Brendan K.
reviews.text: Took my wife for our anniversary — twelve-piece omakase, four pieces sashimi, hot sake, two hours at the bar. The bluefin otoro was the best piece of fish I've eaten in LA in five years. The chef was generous with explanation. We're regulars now.

reviews.name: Yumi P.
reviews.text: Walk in solo any Thursday for the eight-piece set and a small bottle of sake. The same itamae, the same seat, the same uni when it's in. This is a counter-sushi shop, not a roll-shop, and it shows.

contact.mapEmbedQuery: Sushi Nishi Ya, 1712 Victory Blvd, Glendale, CA 91201

seo.title: Sushi Nishi Ya — Counter sushi on Victory Blvd, Glendale
seo.description: A counter-style sushi shop on Victory Boulevard, Glendale. Edomae nigiri, omakase, sashimi, hand rolls. Twelve-seat bar. Reservations recommended.
